metadata.methodology: Official records first. Credible journalism and video transcripts are context only and labelled separately from court/DOJ/FBI/Congress records. Redactions and contact records are not treated as proof of wrongdoing.

official_sources.title: DOJ Epstein Library
official_sources.summary: Central DOJ portal for Epstein Files Transparency Act materials. DOJ notes privacy, victim-protection, and sensitive-material redactions and states the portal may be updated if additional records are identified.

official_sources.title: DOJ Disclosures: Epstein Files Transparency Act
official_sources.summary: Release index covering data sets, court records, FOIA categories, the first phase of declassified files, BOP footage, Maxwell proffer materials, and memoranda/correspondence.

official_sources.title: FOIA: Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
official_sources.summary: DOJ-hosted FBI FOIA records with file IDs EFTA02849279 through EFTA02850658. These are linked for independent verification rather than mirrored wholesale.

official_sources.title: FBI Vault: Jeffrey Epstein
official_sources.access_note: Automated probe received a Cloudflare/403 response, but the URL is the official FBI Vault collection link.
official_sources.summary: Legacy FBI Vault public-record collection. Presented as a linkout source; not treated as exhaustive or identical to newer DOJ/EFTA releases.

official_sources.title: House Oversight Committee release of DOJ-provided Epstein records
official_sources.agency: U.S. House Committee on Oversight and Accountability
official_sources.summary: Committee stated it released 33,295 pages of Epstein-related records provided by DOJ, with redactions for victim identities and child sexual abuse material.

official_sources.title: DOJ publishes 3.5 million+ responsive pages in compliance with EFTA
official_sources.summary: DOJ disclosure index links the January 30, 2026 letter to Congress and publication of at least 3.5 million responsive pages. Treat this as a floor/corpus-size context because later redactions, supplemental files, archive entries, and third-party document indexes can expand the practical research surface.

official_sources.title: Epstein Archive: all documents index
official_sources.summary: Independent public-document index observed with 8,184 document links during the May 25 refresh. OWL links it for discovery/cross-reference coverage while keeping official DOJ/FBI/Congress records and court records as primary sources for claims.

credible_reporting.title: DOJ/FBI say no Epstein client list was found
credible_reporting.use: Claim-check module. Treat as reporting about DOJ/FBI statements, not as an independent audit of all records.

credible_reporting.title: Large DOJ release / roughly 3 million pages context
DOJ allowed lawmakers to review unredacted files
credible_reporting.use: Redactions and controlled congressional access explainer.

credible_reporting.title: FBI records and sex-trafficking conclusions context
DOJ published additional mistakenly withheld files involving uncorroborated claim
credible_reporting.use: Supplemental releases/corrections log with uncorroborated-label language.

release_timeline.event: House Oversight released 33,295 pages of DOJ-provided records
Large DOJ batch reported; corpus described around 3 million pages
Members of Congress allowed controlled review of unredacted files
DOJ supplemental publication of files previously omitted by mistake, with allegations labelled uncorroborated in reporting
DOJ Epstein Library checked as current source hub
DOJ disclosure index showed April 2026 modification date during source refresh checks
